Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: guardrail name validation for Bedrock API (#26)

* fix: sanitize guardrail name and deduplicate retry logic

Bedrock CreateGuardrailCommand throws ValidationException on special
characters (em-dash, accented chars) in the guardrail name. Sanitize
to alphanumeric/hyphens/underscores only. Also extract shared
createResources function to eliminate duplicated retry logic.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: guardrail name must match Bedrock pattern [a-zA-Z0-9][-a-zA-Z0-9]*

Bedrock CreateGuardrail requires names matching ^[a-zA-Z0-9]([-a-zA-Z0-9]*)?$
— no spaces, underscores, or special chars. Replace all invalid chars with
hyphens and collapse consecutive hyphens.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: renumber duplicate migration 014-task-retry-count to 015 (#153)

Both 014-add-notified-at.sql and 014-task-retry-count.sql had the same
prefix. Migration runners apply files in order by name; with two 014s,
only the first alphabetically was applied, silently dropping the
retry_count column addition.

Rename: 014-task-retry-count.sql → 015-task-retry-count.sql

No content changes. Instances that already applied both (via workaround)
should handle this gracefully — the migration runner skips already-applied
migrations by name.

Co-authored-by: BMO <bmo@Davids-Mac-mini.local>

commit.message:
feat: Google OAuth with professional/customer role selection

New Google users are detected in the jwt callback (createdAt < 30s),
given token.needsOnboarding = true, and funnelled to /onboarding before
they can reach any dashboard.

- auth.ts: signIn via Google sets needsOnboarding on new users;
  trigger=update clears it and re-reads role from DB
- proxy.ts: redirect to /onboarding if needsOnboarding is set;
  /onboarding and /api/onboarding bypass role-based guards
- /onboarding: two-card UI (Professional / Customer); calls
  PUT /api/onboarding then updateSession() then pushes to dashboard
- /api/onboarding: PUT updates role in DB for authenticated user
- login page: Google OAuthButton now forwards callbackUrl so existing
  Google users also return to the page they came from

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(doc2): tickle all parent dirs up to music root

The AI subfolder adds an extra level between music root and artist.
Walk up from target to root tickling each intermediate dir (artist,
AI) plus the root itself, so Lidarr sees changes at every level.

commit.message:
v2.48.0: Chrome Extension — Map Tracker Assistant

Chrome extension with side panel that reads the current webpage and shows
contextual Map Tracker data (store info, alerts, visits, route details) with
action buttons that send data back to the app.

Extension:
- Manifest V3 with Side Panel API for persistent sidebar
- Context detection: GlobalWorx alerts, DAO/DSD portal, Google Maps, generic store pages
- Store card, alert status, visit history, route overview
- Action buttons: Record Visit, Accept/Complete Alert, Open in App, View on Map, Copy Store Info

Bridge Server:
- 7 new /api/extension/* endpoints on existing Express server (port 3001)
- SSE streams for real-time state sync between app and extension
- Direct execution of GlobalWorx Puppeteer and WhatsApp actions from extension
- Context lookup by store number or ref number

App Integration:
- extensionBridgeService.js: state push + action listener
- AppContext: debounced state push, SSE action handler
- Vite proxy for /api/extension

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ci): skip :latest retag on executor cache hit (#164)

Moving an existing tag with `gcloud artifacts docker tags add`
requires `artifactregistry.tags.delete` permission, which the WIF
service account lacks. On a cache hit the :latest tag already points
to the same content, so we only need the SHA tag for deploy-staging.

Fixes: PLAT-fyxx

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix last_name defaulting to first_name when only one name provided

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Add security and gh CLI knowledge to built-in user memories

Security category — 6 new rules from real incidents:
- Documentation files leak secrets as dangerously as code
- build.log and .bak files should never be committed
- Camera/IoT tokens are random strings with no prefix pattern
- OAuth Client IDs look like random strings — flag in docs
- Network scan outputs and IP inventory files must never be committed

Git category — 4 new rules from tooling experience:
- Use git-filter-repo for history scrubbing (not BFG, requires Java)
- gh api uses -F (capital) for booleans, -f for strings
- --disable-wiki flag doesn't exist, use gh api -X PATCH
- Branch protection requires JSON input via gh api

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix iPhone photo upload: client-side HEIC conversion + better error display

- Add normalizeImageFile(): converts HEIC/HEIF to JPEG via canvas before
  uploading, so Gemini always receives a supported format. Also downscales
  images wider/taller than 4000px to avoid Gemini size limits.
- Apply normalizeImageFile() in handleFiles before upload/analyze calls,
  using the converted file and updated filename throughout (upload, analyze,
  thumbnail generation).
- Accept files by MIME type in addition to extension, so iOS photos with
  unusual filenames aren't silently dropped.
- Show the actual error message in the progress bar (filename + reason),
  making future failures easier to diagnose.

feat(web): create RealtimeSync system for live factory floor updates (task 118)

Maps Socket.IO domain events (package:created, job:started/completed/stuck/error,
assembly-line:completed) to visual operations in the Phaser factory scene.

- Event buffering until scene is ready with ordered replay
- Per-crate animation queuing via PackageMover integration
- Out-of-order event handling (e.g., completion before start)
- Worker machine state propagation to store and scene
- Crate lifecycle: creation at entrance, movement along conveyor paths, removal on completion
- 47 tests with 100% statement/function coverage

Remove broadcast_log sync that kept overwriting status changes

Two sync blocks (migrations.php and tower.php) ran on every page load
and force-synced platform statuses from broadcast_log, overwriting any
manual changes. Removed both — scheduled_posts is now the sole source
of truth, updated directly by broadcast functions and Repost/Retry.

revert: restore build-go-api to non-race build (#165)

Reverts the -race flag added in #162. The race-instrumented warm step
took 78s vs 24s but only saved ~5s in downstream jobs, making the
critical path 50s slower. The non-race build serves its purpose as a
compile gate and module cache warmer.

commit.message:
Rebrand page titles from DBT to acidtest.io

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Rewrite company insights to use Claude instead of brittle heuristics

Old approach made 8 NewsAPI calls with narrow keyword matching, returning
null for most companies. New approach: 1 news fetch + 1 Claude call to
extract growth, funding, tech stack, and hiring signals reliably.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: truncate job descriptions to 800 chars to avoid Gemini TPM limit

On first run with 50-100 new jobs, full HTML descriptions were blowing
out the free-tier tokens-per-minute quota. Truncating at 800 chars
reduces prompt size ~10x while preserving enough for accurate scoring.

Documents the issue and fix in README Troubleshooting section and
adds a pointer in CLAUDE.md Known Issues.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Implement conveyor belt pipeline — all steps run concurrently

Scrape, AI, watermark, CDN upload, publish all run in parallel.
Each video flows through pipeline independently: video 1 goes to AI
while video 2 is still downloading. One-shot steps (scrape) run
concurrently with polling workers via runOnceWorker().

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(agent-v2): classify tab usa decision real, no clasificacion de Claude

El Classify tab mostraba SILENCIOSO para saludos porque mapeaba
la category de Claude (irrelevante) en vez de la decision de Capa 3.
Ahora usa decision.action: silence→SILENCIOSO, handoff→HANDOFF,
respond/create_order→RESPONDIBLE.

defense-in-depth rationale, threat model table, explicit limitations
(no authentication, nonce misuse risk), Python prototype caveats,
and the T-m security margin enforcement with code examples.

commit.message:
feat: replace brag seed with real work history and fix timeline year filter

- Rewrote seed-brag.ts with 51 real entries from 3 years of work (Jun 2023–Feb 2026)
- Replaced generic placeholder categories with 6 work-type categories:
  Feature Development, Bug Fixes & Maintenance, Performance & SEO,
  Payments & Fintech, Web3 / Blockchain, Cross-Platform & i18n
- Entries sourced from actual git commit history across 9 projects
- Plain-language descriptions readable by both technical and non-technical viewers
- No company names; 6 pinned highlights selected for maximum impact
- Fix BragTimeline year/category filter: add key prop to stagger container
  so Framer Motion remounts and re-animates when filters change (entries
  were rendered but invisible at opacity:0 due to whileInView once:true
  not re-triggering variant propagation for newly mounted children)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
docs: add architecture.md — block diagram and module data-flow

Full Mermaid block diagram showing the 8-module pipeline, followed
by six step-by-step sections covering each stage: input/salt
derivation, Collatz iteration (with parity table), PSC-QOWF masking
(with security note admonition), key schedule derivation pipeline,
ChaCha20 round function, and XOR encryption.  Includes the initial
state layout table and module responsibility summary.

pitch sequence timeline.

commit.message:
feat(web): add onboarding wizard and redesign settings page

Add a multi-step onboarding flow that guides new users through platform
selection, AI key setup, extension installation, account connections,
and niche configuration. Root route now redirects to onboarding or
dashboard based on localStorage completion flag.

New components: OnboardingRedirect, MaskedInput, NicheSelector, and 11
onboarding step pages (Welcome, Platforms, AI Keys, Install Extension,
Connect X/LinkedIn/GitHub, Niches, Done). Includes useOnboarding hook
with step management and localStorage persistence.

Also redesigns the Settings page and adds an Onboarding link to the
sidebar navigation.
